entities.name: Sam Bourton
entities.context: Founder Retreat Curator & Mentor Network builder; curates high-quality founder cohorts for retreats and mentorship

entities.name: McKinsey
entities.context: Consulting firm where Sam worked for 8 years (implied from context); experienced multiple firm eras including covid lull and restructuring

entities.name: Founder Retreat
entities.context: Sam's cohort program providing mentorship and peer learning for founders at various stages

entities.name: Cohort Curation
entities.context: Sam's approach to thoughtfully mixing technical and business founders, early-stage and experienced, for mutual learning

entities.name: Optimal Performance Dynamics
entities.context: Sam's belief that teams perform best with 'just a little bit too much work' - learning fast, moving in right direction

entities.name: Peer Learning
entities.context: Core method in Sam's retreats; founders at different stages and functions learning from each other

entities.name: Team Morale Management
entities.context: Key challenge Sam navigates - balancing engagement when work is unbalanced (too much or too little)

relationships.context: Previously worked at consulting firm for 8 years before current founder work
Curates and runs the retreat program for founder cohorts
Core methodology for ensuring diversity benefits all participants
Articulated sophisticated view on sweet spot of workload for team performance
Foundation of retreat model - cross-stage, cross-function founder learning
Curation enables effective peer learning by ensuring diverse experience profiles
Both concern managing team engagement and performance balance
